subject: [RFC PATCH 23/45] mm: page_alloc: add CONFIG_DEBUG_VM sanity checks for SPB counters

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch adds sanity checks to the page allocation code in the Linux kernel to ensure that per-superpageblock counters (nr_free, nr_unmovable, nr_reclaimable, and nr_movable) do not exceed the total number of pageblocks. The checks are only enabled when CONFIG_DEBUG_VM is set, which allows for early detection of counter drift during stress testing without affecting production performance. The patch includes a new function spb_debug_check() that performs these checks and is called after each operation that mutates these counters. Several real bugs in the SPB stack were caught by this check during development, making it a valuable addition to the kernel code.
